[[JA:二宮神社 (船橋市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11371511}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 二宮神社 |画像 = [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) shaden.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}三山5丁目20-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|建速須佐之男命}}
{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|櫛稲田比売命}}
{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}
{{jalink|藤原時平|Q2994735|藤原時平命}}
{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}}
{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
（伝）{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 伝・{{jalink|弘仁|Q1373070}}年間（{{jalink|810年|Q23819}}-{{jalink|824年|Q30353}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|権現造|Q3155268}} |別名 = 寒川神社、三山明神、二宮明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |神事 = {{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=二宮神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|鳥居]] '''二宮神社'''（にのみやじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}三山にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}で、{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}と伝える。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。古くは'''三山明神'''あるいは'''二宮明神'''とも称された『千葉県の地名』二宮神社項。。 === 祭神 === 祭神は神社由緒書に従って記載。 * '''{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|建速須佐之男命}}''' * '''{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|櫛稲田比売命}}''' * '''{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}''' * '''{{jalink|藤原時平|Q2994735|藤原時平命}}''' :伝承によると、{{jalink|治承|Q1194671}}4年（{{jalink|1180年|Q19702}}）に左遷された{{jalink|藤原師経|Q11623227}}がこの地に住みついた際に祖先の藤原時平を相殿に合せ祀った。時平は{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}（天神）の政敵であるため、天神の氏子は当社に参拝しなかったと伝わる。 * '''{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}}''' :{{jalink|明治時代}}に近隣の無格社・若宮八幡神社を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}。 * '''{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}}''' :明治時代に摂社・阿波八幡神社を合祀。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|弘仁|Q1373070}}年間（{{jalink|810年|Q23819}}～{{jalink|824年|Q30353}}）、{{jalink|嵯峨天皇|Q316654}}の勅命により創建されたという神社由緒書。。地名同様「三山」の名は古くは「御山」と見え、当社に起因する。{{jalink|三山氏|Q11355183}}が先祖代々{{jalink|神官|Q11589454}}を務めた『千葉県の地名』三山村項。。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}には「{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|千葉郡|Q5095301}} 寒川神社」の記載があるが、当社はその{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}とされている。また、{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}の{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}として朝廷からの崇敬をうけたという（ただし{{jalink|旭市|Q637868}}の{{jalink|玉崎神社|Q11572538}}も二宮を称する）。二宮神社と呼ばれ始めた時期は不明であるが、{{jalink|乾元 (日本)|Q1327472|乾元}}2年（{{jalink|1303年|Q5614}}）銘の梵鐘（成田市祥鳳院蔵）に「総州二宮社壇」とあることから{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}にはすでにこの呼称があったとうかがえる。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年（{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}）には{{jalink|徳川家康|Q171977}}から{{jalink|朱印地・黒印地|Q11521520|朱印地}}10石を与えられたほか、{{jalink|江戸幕府|Q205662}}将軍からの寄進は幾度にも及んだ。{{jalink|安政|Q1327259}}5年（1858年）刊の『成田参詣記』では「三山明神社（みやまみょうじんやしろ）」の図の挿絵とともに紹介されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}に入ると、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した。明治43年（1910年）に若宮八幡神社と元摂社・阿波八幡神社を合祀した。 古来より「注連下」（氏子の区域）は21・3ヶ村（現在の{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}東部・北部、{{jalink|習志野市|Q318047}}、{{jalink|八千代市|Q328651}}、{{jalink|千葉市|Q170616}}西部）にまたがる広大な地域に達し、現在でも例祭などで、その影響力を見ることができる。 === 境内 === === 社殿 === 現在の社殿は{{jalink|安永}}年間（{{jalink|1772年|Q7690}}〜{{jalink|1781年|Q7719}}）に再建されたものである。本殿・拝殿とも屋根は銅板葺であるが、本殿は{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}11年（{{jalink|1922年|Q2165}}）10月に、拝殿は大正14年（{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}）に{{jalink|茅葺|Q874531}}屋根から葺き替えられた。この社殿の平面形式は{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に流行した{{jalink|権現造|Q3155268}}であるが、{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}にいたる床面の高さは同じになるようになっている。また、{{jalink|軒|Q11637472}}の{{jalink|組物|Q7814068}}及び腰組も当時の正規の{{jalink|宮大工|Q65261787}}の手法によって意匠と造作がなされている。昭和53年（{{jalink|1978年|Q2483}}）{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}に{{jalink|船橋市指定文化財一覧|Q11614137|船橋市の有形文化財}}に指定された。 File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) haiden.JPG|拝殿 ファイル:ninomiyajinnjya7.jpg|前広場 ファイル:Ninomiya.jpg|1918年の社殿写真 === 参道 === [[ファイル:Ninomiya-Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|参道]] 正面の鳥居から参拝すると参道はいったん低くなり、谷を横断して進むように作られている。なお、谷の底部には小川が流れており、その水脈は{{jalink|習志野市|Q318047}}の{{jalink|津田沼|Q7849928}}に鎮座している{{jalink|菊田神社|Q11619900}}境内にある池へとつながっているといわれている。谷底を直線に進むと石段をのぼりおわる頃に拝殿の向拝の唐破風が見えてくるようになっており、大きく雄大な{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}の曲線、そして雄渾な彫刻をゆっくりと鑑賞できるようにと大工棟梁の{{jalink|デザイン|Q82604}}の工夫が凝らされている。 === 祭事 === * {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}}（1月1日） * 御射初祭（1月7日） * お筒粥炊き神事（1月14日） * {{jalink|節分|Q746798|節分祭}}（2月3日） * '''例祭'''（10月16日） * '''大火揚'''（おおびよ、{{jalink|旧暦|Q11511066}}11月13日の夜） :当地に流されたと言われる藤原師経らを迎えようとして里人がかがり火を焚いたことに因むまつり。 * '''{{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}}'''（丑年・未年） :当社を中心とし、船橋市・{{jalink|千葉市|Q170616}}・{{jalink|八千代市|Q328651}}・習志野市の9神社が参加する、数え年で7年に1度の祭り。当社は「父」の役割を務める。 === 周辺 === * '''{{jalink|神宮寺 (船橋市)|Q11589481|醫王山神宮寺}}''' :隣接。{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}以前の当社{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}。 === 文化財 === === 千葉県指定無形民俗文化財 === * {{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}} - 平成16年3月30日指定。 === 船橋市指定文化財 === * 社殿（有形文化財（建造物）） - 平成4年2月27日指定。 * {{jalink|斎藤園女}}等奉納句額（有形文化財（歴史資料）） - 昭和55年10月23日指定。 * 二宮神社神楽（無形民俗文化財） - 平成7年6月26日指定。 * 二宮神社のイチョウ（天然記念物） - 平成24年3月30日指定。 === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|津田沼駅|Q801534}}（{{jalink|東日本旅客鉄道|Q499071|JR東日本}}{{jalink|総武本線|Q1191364}}）北口から ** {{jalink|京成バス|Q11374367}}（津02 二宮神社行）で終点「二宮神社」バス停下車（乗車時間20分） ** {{jalink|船橋新京成バス}}（津08 二宮神社前行）で終点「二宮神社前」バス停下車（乗車時間20分） * {{jalink|薬園台駅|Q8047226}}（{{jalink|京成松戸線|Q3553734}}）から ** 徒歩（3分）で国道296号線の「薬園台駅入口」バス停から、{{jalink|船橋新京成バス}}（津08 二宮神社前行）で終点「二宮神社前」バス停下車（乗車時間10分） * {{jalink|京成大久保駅|Q6383851}}（{{jalink|京成本線|Q1063675}}）から ** 徒歩（7分）で「東邦大学前」バス停から、{{jalink|京成バス|Q11374367}}（津02 二宮神社行）で終点「二宮神社」バス停下車（乗車時間6分） *: 京成大久保駅付近の京成バスは下り・上りで経路が異なる。下りの最寄りは「東邦大学前」バス停、上りの最寄りは「大久保十字路」バス停。 === 出典 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名体系 千葉県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）船橋市 二宮神社項 * 『絵はがき写真に残された明治～大正～昭和-』（船橋市郷土資料館、平成17年3月23日） * 『祝市制施行70周年～船橋の100年～』（郷土出版社、平成19年2月5日） === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|三山氏|Q11355183}} * {{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}} * {{jalink|菊田神社|Q11619900}} * {{jalink|寒川神社 (千葉市)|Q11457393|寒川神社}} （千葉県{{jalink|千葉市|Q170616}}{{jalink|中央区 (千葉市)|Q1075869|中央区}}） - 当社と同じく式内社・寒川神社の論社 * {{jalink|玉崎神社|Q11572538}} （千葉県{{jalink|旭市|Q637868}}） - 下総国二宮を称するもう1つの神社 * [[JA:二宮神社 (船橋市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11371511}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 二宮神社 |画像 = [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) shaden.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}三山5丁目20-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|建速須佐之男命}}
{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|櫛稲田比売命}}
{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}

{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}}
{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
（伝）{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 伝・{{jalink|弘仁|Q1373070}}年間（{{jalink|810年|Q23819}}-{{jalink|824年|Q30353}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|権現造|Q3155268}} |別名 = 寒川神社、三山明神、二宮明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |神事 = {{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=二宮神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|鳥居]] '''二宮神社'''（にのみやじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}三山にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}で、{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}と伝える。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。古くは'''三山明神'''あるいは'''二宮明神'''とも称された『千葉県の地名』二宮神社項。。 === 祭神 === 祭神は神社由緒書に従って記載。 * '''{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|建速須佐之男命}}''' * '''{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|櫛稲田比売命}}''' * '''{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}''' * '''{{jalink|藤原時平|Q2994735|藤原時平命}}''' :伝承によると、{{jalink|治承|Q1194671}}4年（{{jalink|1180年|Q19702}}）に左遷された{{jalink|藤原師経|Q11623227}}がこの地に住みついた際に祖先の藤原時平を相殿に合せ祀った。時平は{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}（天神）の政敵であるため、天神の氏子は当社に参拝しなかったと伝わる。 * '''{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}}''' :{{jalink|明治時代}}に近隣の無格社・若宮八幡神社を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}。 * '''{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}}''' :明治時代に摂社・阿波八幡神社を合祀。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|弘仁|Q1373070}}年間（{{jalink|810年|Q23819}}～{{jalink|824年|Q30353}}）、{{jalink|嵯峨天皇|Q316654}}の勅命により創建されたという神社由緒書。。地名同様「三山」の名は古くは「御山」と見え、当社に起因する。{{jalink|三山氏|Q11355183}}が先祖代々{{jalink|神官|Q11589454}}を務めた『千葉県の地名』三山村項。。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}には「{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|千葉郡|Q5095301}} 寒川神社」の記載があるが、当社はその{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}とされている。また、{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}の{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}として朝廷からの崇敬をうけたという（ただし{{jalink|旭市|Q637868}}の{{jalink|玉崎神社|Q11572538}}も二宮を称する）。二宮神社と呼ばれ始めた時期は不明であるが、{{jalink|乾元 (日本)|Q1327472|乾元}}2年（{{jalink|1303年|Q5614}}）銘の梵鐘（成田市祥鳳院蔵）に「総州二宮社壇」とあることから{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}にはすでにこの呼称があったとうかがえる。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年（{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}）には{{jalink|徳川家康|Q171977}}から{{jalink|朱印地・黒印地|Q11521520|朱印地}}10石を与えられたほか、{{jalink|江戸幕府|Q205662}}将軍からの寄進は幾度にも及んだ。{{jalink|安政|Q1327259}}5年（1858年）刊の『成田参詣記』では「三山明神社（みやまみょうじんやしろ）」の図の挿絵とともに紹介されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}に入ると、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した。明治43年（1910年）に若宮八幡神社と元摂社・阿波八幡神社を合祀した。 古来より「注連下」（氏子の区域）は21・3ヶ村（現在の{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}東部・北部、{{jalink|習志野市|Q318047}}、{{jalink|八千代市|Q328651}}、{{jalink|千葉市|Q170616}}西部）にまたがる広大な地域に達し、現在でも例祭などで、その影響力を見ることができる。 === 境内 === === 社殿 === 現在の社殿は{{jalink|安永}}年間（{{jalink|1772年|Q7690}}〜{{jalink|1781年|Q7719}}）に再建されたものである。本殿・拝殿とも屋根は銅板葺であるが、本殿は{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}11年（{{jalink|1922年|Q2165}}）10月に、拝殿は大正14年（{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}）に{{jalink|茅葺|Q874531}}屋根から葺き替えられた。この社殿の平面形式は{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に流行した{{jalink|権現造|Q3155268}}であるが、{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}にいたる床面の高さは同じになるようになっている。また、{{jalink|軒|Q11637472}}の{{jalink|組物|Q7814068}}及び腰組も当時の正規の{{jalink|宮大工|Q65261787}}の手法によって意匠と造作がなされている。昭和53年（{{jalink|1978年|Q2483}}）{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}に{{jalink|船橋市指定文化財一覧|Q11614137|船橋市の有形文化財}}に指定された。 File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) haiden.JPG|拝殿 ファイル:ninomiyajinnjya7.jpg|前広場 ファイル:Ninomiya.jpg|1918年の社殿写真 === 参道 === [[ファイル:Ninomiya-Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|参道]] 正面の鳥居から参拝すると参道はいったん低くなり、谷を横断して進むように作られている。なお、谷の底部には小川が流れており、その水脈は{{jalink|習志野市|Q318047}}の{{jalink|津田沼|Q7849928}}に鎮座している{{jalink|菊田神社|Q11619900}}境内にある池へとつながっているといわれている。谷底を直線に進むと石段をのぼりおわる頃に拝殿の向拝の唐破風が見えてくるようになっており、大きく雄大な{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}の曲線、そして雄渾な彫刻をゆっくりと鑑賞できるようにと大工棟梁の{{jalink|デザイン|Q82604}}の工夫が凝らされている。 === 祭事 === * {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}}（1月1日） * 御射初祭（1月7日） * お筒粥炊き神事（1月14日） * {{jalink|節分|Q746798|節分祭}}（2月3日） * '''例祭'''（10月16日） * '''大火揚'''（おおびよ、{{jalink|旧暦|Q11511066}}11月13日の夜） :当地に流されたと言われる藤原師経らを迎えようとして里人がかがり火を焚いたことに因むまつり。 * '''{{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}}'''（丑年・未年） :当社を中心とし、船橋市・{{jalink|千葉市|Q170616}}・{{jalink|八千代市|Q328651}}・習志野市の9神社が参加する、数え年で7年に1度の祭り。当社は「父」の役割を務める。 === 周辺 === * '''{{jalink|神宮寺 (船橋市)|Q11589481|醫王山神宮寺}}''' :隣接。{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}以前の当社{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}。 === 文化財 === === 千葉県指定無形民俗文化財 === * {{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}} - 平成16年3月30日指定。 === 船橋市指定文化財 === * 社殿（有形文化財（建造物）） - 平成4年2月27日指定。 * {{jalink|斎藤園女}}等奉納句額（有形文化財（歴史資料）） - 昭和55年10月23日指定。 * 二宮神社神楽（無形民俗文化財） - 平成7年6月26日指定。 * 二宮神社のイチョウ（天然記念物） - 平成24年3月30日指定。 === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|津田沼駅|Q801534}}（{{jalink|東日本旅客鉄道|Q499071|JR東日本}}{{jalink|総武本線|Q1191364}}）北口から ** {{jalink|京成バス|Q11374367}}（津02 二宮神社行）で終点「二宮神社」バス停下車（乗車時間20分） ** {{jalink|船橋新京成バス}}（津08 二宮神社前行）で終点「二宮神社前」バス停下車（乗車時間20分） * {{jalink|薬園台駅|Q8047226}}（{{jalink|京成松戸線|Q3553734}}）から ** 徒歩（3分）で国道296号線の「薬園台駅入口」バス停から、{{jalink|船橋新京成バス}}（津08 二宮神社前行）で終点「二宮神社前」バス停下車（乗車時間10分） * {{jalink|京成大久保駅|Q6383851}}（{{jalink|京成本線|Q1063675}}）から ** 徒歩（7分）で「東邦大学前」バス停から、{{jalink|京成バス|Q11374367}}（津02 二宮神社行）で終点「二宮神社」バス停下車（乗車時間6分） *: 京成大久保駅付近の京成バスは下り・上りで経路が異なる。下りの最寄りは「東邦大学前」バス停、上りの最寄りは「大久保十字路」バス停。 === 出典 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名体系 千葉県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）船橋市 二宮神社項 * [[JA:二宮神社 (船橋市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11371511}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 二宮神社 |画像 = [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) shaden.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}三山5丁目20-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|建速須佐之男命}}
{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|櫛稲田比売命}}
{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}
{{jalink|藤原時平|Q2994735|藤原時平命}}
{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}}
{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
（伝）{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 伝・{{jalink|弘仁|Q1373070}}年間（{{jalink|810年|Q23819}}-{{jalink|824年|Q30353}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|権現造|Q3155268}} |別名 = 寒川神社、三山明神、二宮明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |神事 = {{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=二宮神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|鳥居]] '''二宮神社'''（にのみやじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|千葉県|Q80011}}{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}三山にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}で、{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}と伝える。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。古くは'''三山明神'''あるいは'''二宮明神'''とも称された『千葉県の地名』二宮神社項。。 === 祭神 === 祭神は神社由緒書に従って記載。 * '''{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|建速須佐之男命}}''' * '''{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|櫛稲田比売命}}''' * '''{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}''' * '''{{jalink|藤原時平|Q2994735|藤原時平命}}''' :伝承によると、{{jalink|治承|Q1194671}}4年（{{jalink|1180年|Q19702}}）に左遷された{{jalink|藤原師経|Q11623227}}がこの地に住みついた際に祖先の藤原時平を相殿に合せ祀った。時平は{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}（天神）の政敵であるため、天神の氏子は当社に参拝しなかったと伝わる。 * '''{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}}''' :{{jalink|明治時代}}に近隣の無格社・若宮八幡神社を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}。 * '''{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}}''' :明治時代に摂社・阿波八幡神社を合祀。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|弘仁|Q1373070}}年間（{{jalink|810年|Q23819}}～{{jalink|824年|Q30353}}）、{{jalink|嵯峨天皇|Q316654}}の勅命により創建されたという神社由緒書。。地名同様「三山」の名は古くは「御山」と見え、当社に起因する。{{jalink|三山氏|Q11355183}}が先祖代々{{jalink|神官|Q11589454}}を務めた『千葉県の地名』三山村項。。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}には「{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}{{jalink|千葉郡|Q5095301}} 寒川神社」の記載があるが、当社はその{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}とされている。また、{{jalink|下総国|Q1053695}}の{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|二宮}}として朝廷からの崇敬をうけたという（ただし{{jalink|旭市|Q637868}}の{{jalink|玉崎神社|Q11572538}}も二宮を称する）。二宮神社と呼ばれ始めた時期は不明であるが、{{jalink|乾元 (日本)|Q1327472|乾元}}2年（{{jalink|1303年|Q5614}}）銘の梵鐘（成田市祥鳳院蔵）に「総州二宮社壇」とあることから{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}にはすでにこの呼称があったとうかがえる。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年（{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}）には{{jalink|徳川家康|Q171977}}から{{jalink|朱印地・黒印地|Q11521520|朱印地}}10石を与えられたほか、{{jalink|江戸幕府|Q205662}}将軍からの寄進は幾度にも及んだ。{{jalink|安政|Q1327259}}5年（1858年）刊の『成田参詣記』では「三山明神社（みやまみょうじんやしろ）」の図の挿絵とともに紹介されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}に入ると、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した。明治43年（1910年）に若宮八幡神社と元摂社・阿波八幡神社を合祀した。 古来より「注連下」（氏子の区域）は21・3ヶ村（現在の{{jalink|船橋市|Q271417}}東部・北部、{{jalink|習志野市|Q318047}}、{{jalink|八千代市|Q328651}}、{{jalink|千葉市|Q170616}}西部）にまたがる広大な地域に達し、現在でも例祭などで、その影響力を見ることができる。 === 境内 === === 社殿 === 現在の社殿は{{jalink|安永}}年間（{{jalink|1772年|Q7690}}〜{{jalink|1781年|Q7719}}）に再建されたものである。本殿・拝殿とも屋根は銅板葺であるが、本殿は{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}11年（{{jalink|1922年|Q2165}}）10月に、拝殿は大正14年（{{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}）に{{jalink|茅葺|Q874531}}屋根から葺き替えられた。この社殿の平面形式は{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に流行した{{jalink|権現造|Q3155268}}であるが、{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}にいたる床面の高さは同じになるようになっている。また、{{jalink|軒|Q11637472}}の{{jalink|組物|Q7814068}}及び腰組も当時の正規の{{jalink|宮大工|Q65261787}}の手法によって意匠と造作がなされている。昭和53年（{{jalink|1978年|Q2483}}）{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}に{{jalink|船橋市指定文化財一覧|Q11614137|船橋市の有形文化財}}に指定された。 File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) haiden.JPG|拝殿 ファイル:ninomiyajinnjya7.jpg|前広場 ファイル:Ninomiya.jpg|1918年の社殿写真 === 参道 === [[ファイル:Ninomiya-Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|参道]] 正面の鳥居から参拝すると参道はいったん低くなり、谷を横断して進むように作られている。なお、谷の底部には小川が流れており、その水脈は{{jalink|習志野市|Q318047}}の{{jalink|津田沼|Q7849928}}に鎮座している{{jalink|菊田神社|Q11619900}}境内にある池へとつながっているといわれている。谷底を直線に進むと石段をのぼりおわる頃に拝殿の向拝の唐破風が見えてくるようになっており、大きく雄大な{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}の曲線、そして雄渾な彫刻をゆっくりと鑑賞できるようにと大工棟梁の{{jalink|デザイン|Q82604}}の工夫が凝らされている。 === 祭事 === * {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}}（1月1日） * 御射初祭（1月7日） * お筒粥炊き神事（1月14日） * {{jalink|節分|Q746798|節分祭}}（2月3日） * '''例祭'''（10月16日） * '''大火揚'''（おおびよ、{{jalink|旧暦|Q11511066}}11月13日の夜） :当地に流されたと言われる藤原師経らを迎えようとして里人がかがり火を焚いたことに因むまつり。 * '''{{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}}'''（丑年・未年） :当社を中心とし、船橋市・{{jalink|千葉市|Q170616}}・{{jalink|八千代市|Q328651}}・習志野市の9神社が参加する、数え年で7年に1度の祭り。当社は「父」の役割を務める。 === 周辺 === * '''{{jalink|神宮寺 (船橋市)|Q11589481|醫王山神宮寺}}''' :隣接。{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}以前の当社{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}。 === 文化財 === === 千葉県指定無形民俗文化財 === * {{jalink|下総三山の七年祭り|Q11361280}} - 平成16年3月30日指定。 === 船橋市指定文化財 === * 社殿（有形文化財（建造物）） - 平成4年2月27日指定。 * {{jalink|斎藤園女}}等奉納句額（有形文化財（歴史資料）） - 昭和55年10月23日指定。 * 二宮神社神楽（無形民俗文化財） - 平成7年6月26日指定。 * 二宮神社のイチョウ（天然記念物） - 平成24年3月30日指定。 === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|津田沼駅|Q801534}}（{{jalink|東日本旅客鉄道|Q499071|JR東日本}}{{jalink|総武本線|Q1191364}}）北口から ** {{jalink|京成バス|Q11374367}}（津02 二宮神社行）で終点「二宮神社」バス停下車（乗車時間20分） ** {{jalink|船橋新京成バス}}（津08 二宮神社前行）で終点「二宮神社前」バス停下車（乗車時間20分） * {{jalink|薬園台駅|Q8047226}}（{{jalink|京成松戸線|Q3553734}}）から ** 徒歩（3分）で国道296号線の「薬園台駅入口」バス停から、{{jalink|船橋新京成バス}}（津08 二宮神社前行）で終点「二宮神社前」バス停下車（乗車時間10分） * {{jalink|京成大久保駅|Q6383851}}（{{jalink|京成本線|Q1063675}}）から ** 徒歩（7分）で「東邦大学前」バス停から、{{jalink|京成バス|Q11374367}}（津02 二宮神社行）で終点「二宮神社」バス停下車（乗車時間6分） *: 京成大久保駅付近の京成バスは下り・上りで経路が異なる。下りの最寄りは「東邦大学前」バス停、上りの最寄りは「大久保十字路」バス停。 === 出典 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名体系 千葉県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）船橋市 二宮神社項 * Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend・{{jalink|Honin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810 years|Q23819}}-{{jalink|824 years|Q30353}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Gongenzo|Q3155268}} |Other names = Samukawa Shrine, Miyama Myojin, Ninomiya Myojin|Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}} |Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Shimosa Sanzan Seven Year Festival|Q11361280}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ninomiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]]'''Ninomiya Shrine'' (Ninomiya Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} Miyama. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, tell them {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. In ancient times, it was also called ``Miyama Myojin'' or ``Ninomiya Myojin''. . === Enshrined God === The enshrined deity is listed according to the shrine history book. * '''{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kenhaya Susano no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Kushinada Hime|Q505469|Kushiinada Hime no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Fujiwara Tokihira|Q2994735|Fujiwara Tokihira Mikoto}}''' :According to legend, when {{jalink|Fujiwara no Moritsune|Q11623227}}, who was demoted in 4th year ({{jalink|1180|Q19702}}), settled in this area, he enshrined his ancestor, Fujiwara Tokihira, in his side hall. Since Tokihira is a political enemy of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (Tenjin), it is said that the parishioners of Tenjin did not visit the shrine . * '''{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Daisaku no Mikoto}}''' :{{jalink|Meiji period}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, a nearby mukakusha, was {{jalink|together enshrined|Q11412524}}. * '''{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Hondabetsu no Mikoto}}''' :In the Meiji period, Setsusha and Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. === History === According to the shrine's legend, the shrine history book was founded by the imperial order of {{jalink|Emperor Saga|Q316654}} during the {{jalink|Konin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810|Q23819}} to {{jalink|824|Q30353}}). . Like place names, the name ``Miyama'' appeared to be ``Miyama'' in ancient times, and was attributed to our company. {{jalink|Miyama clan|Q11355183}} served as {{jalink|priests|Q11589454}} for generations. ``Chiba Prefecture place name'' Miyama Village. .``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}} {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} states: Samukawa Shrine", but our company is said to be {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. It is also said that it was revered by the imperial court as {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}} of {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}} (however, {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} of {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}} is also called Ninomiya). It is unknown when it began to be called Ninomiya Shrine, but (Japan)|Q1327472|Inuigen}} 2 ({{jalink|1303|Q5614}}) The temple bell (stored in Shohoin, Narita City) says ``Soshu Ninomiya Shadan'', which suggests that this name already existed in the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}.{{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 19 years ({{jalink|1591|Q6766}}), {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} gave {{jalink|shuinji/black seals|Q11521520|shuinji}} 10 koku and , {{jalink|Edo Shogunate|Q205662}}The shogun made donations many times. {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}It is introduced in ``Narita Pilgrimage Record'' published in 1858 along with an illustration of ``Miyama Myo Shrine''. After entering {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1910, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Gensetsusha Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. Since ancient times, "Shirenshita" (area of parishioners) has consisted of 21.3 villages (currently {{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} eastern and northern areas, {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}, {{ja link|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}), and its influence can still be seen today at annual festivals. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The current shrine building was rebuilt during the {{jalink|Yasunaga}} years ({{jalink|1772|Q7690}} to {{jalink|1781|Q7719}}). Both the main shrine and worship hall have copper plate roofs, but the main shrine was built in {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}11 ({{jalink|1922|Q2165}})1 In October, the worship hall had its {{jalink|thatched roof|Q874531}} roof replaced in Taisho 14 ({{jalink|1925|Q18107}}). The floor plan of this shrine is {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}}, which was popular during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, but the floor height from {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}} to {{jalink|haiden|Q3076699}} is the same. In addition, the {{jalink|eaves|Q11637472}}'s {{jalink|kumimono|Q7814068}} and koshigumi were designed and constructed using the regular {{jalink|miya carpenter|Q65261787}} method of the time. In 1978 ({{jalink|1978|Q2483}}) {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}, it was designated as {{jalink|List of Funabashi City Designated Cultural Properties|Q11614137|Funabashi City's Tangible Cultural Properties}}. File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) haiden.JPG|Shrine of worshipFile:ninomiyajinnjya7.jpg|Mae Square File: Ninomiya.jpg|Photo of the shrine building in 1918 === Approach === [[File:Ninomiya-Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sando]] When you approach the shrine from the torii gate in front of the shrine, the approach becomes lower and then crosses the valley. Furthermore, a stream flows at the bottom of the valley, and the water vein is said to lead to a pond within the grounds of {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}}, which is enshrined in {{jalink|Tsudanuma|Q7849928}} in {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}. If you walk along the valley floor in a straight line, by the time you reach the top of the stone steps, you will be able to see the Karahafu facing the worship hall, and the master carpenter's {{jalink|Design|Q82604}} has been ingenious so that you can take your time to admire the large, majestic {{jalink|Karagohafu|Q977293}} curves and the majestic carvings. === Festival === * {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} (January 1st) * Oshahatsu Festival (January 7th) * Otsutsu porridge cooking ritual (January 14th) * {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} (February 3rd) * '''Regular Festival''' (October 16th) * '''Daikaage'' (Obiyo, {{jalink|lunar calendar|Q11511066}}night of November 13th) :A festival named after the bonfires lit by villagers to welcome Fujiwara no Moritsune and others who were said to have been exiled to this area. * '''{{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}}''' (Year of the Ox/Year of the Wild) :A festival held once every seven years, centered around our company, with nine shrines in Funabashi City, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}, {{jalink|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, and Narashino City participating. Our company plays the role of "father." === Surroundings === * '''{{jalink|Jinguji Temple (Funabashi City)|Q11589481|Douzan Jinguji Temple}}''' : Adjacent. Our company {{jalink|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}} before {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. === Cultural assets === === Chiba Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} - Designated on March 30, 2004. === Cultural property designated by Funabashi City === * Shrine building (tangible cultural property (building)) - Designated on February 27, 1992. * {{jalink|Saito Sonojo}} etc. dedicated haiku plaque (tangible cultural property (historical material)) - Designated on October 23, 1980. * Ninomiya Shrine Kagura (Intangible Folk Cultural Property) - Designated on June 26, 1995. * Ginkgo at Ninomiya Shrine (natural monument) - Designated on March 30, 2012. === Transportation access === * {{jalink|Tsudanuma Station|Q801534}} ({{jalink|East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|JR East}}{{jalink|Sobu Main Line|Q1191364}}) From the north exit ** Take the {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya Shrine" (ride time: 20 minutes) ** Take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya-jinja-mae" (20 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Yakuendai Station|Q8047226}} ({{jalink|Keisei Matsudo Line|Q3553734}}) ** Walk (3 minutes) from the Yakuendai Station Iriguchi bus stop on National Route 296, take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya-jinja-mae (10 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Keisei Okubo Station|Q6383851}} ({{jalink|Keisei Main Line|Q1063675}}) ** Walk (7 minutes) from the Toho Daigaku-mae bus stop, take {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya Shrine (ride time: 6 minutes) *: Keisei buses near Keisei Okubo Station have different routes for going down and going up. The nearest bus stop for going down is "Toho Daigaku-mae" and the nearest bus stop for going up is "Okubo Jujiro". === Source === {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System Chiba Prefecture Place Names" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Funabashi City Ninomiya Shrine Section * “Meiji, Taisho, and Showa eras preserved in postcard photographs” (Funabashi City Local Museum, March 23, 2005) * “Celebrating the 70th Anniversary of the Municipal System - 100 Years of Funabashi” (Kyoto Publishing, February 5, 2007) === Related items === * {{jalink|Mr. Miyama|Q11355183}} * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} * {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}} * {{jalink|Samukawa Shrine (Chiba City)|Q11457393|Samukawa Shrine}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}{{jalink|Chuo Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|Chuo Ward}}) - Like our company, Shikinai Shrine and Samukawa Shrine Ronsha * {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}}) - Another shrine named Shimousa Province Ninomiya * {{jalink|Gozu Tenno|Q11570247}} === External link === {{commonscat|Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi)}} * [http://www.ninomiya-jinja.jp Ninomiya Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ninomiya Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下総国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:嵯峨天皇]] [[Category:藤原時平]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}[[Category:qq]]

Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend・{{jalink|Honin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810 years|Q23819}}-{{jalink|824 years|Q30353}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Gongenzo|Q3155268}} |Other names = Samukawa Shrine, Miyama Myojin, Ninomiya Myojin|Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}} |Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Shimosa Sanzan Seven Year Festival|Q11361280}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ninomiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]]'''Ninomiya Shrine'' (Ninomiya Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} Miyama. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, tell them {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. In ancient times, it was also called ``Miyama Myojin'' or ``Ninomiya Myojin''. . === Enshrined God === The enshrined deity is listed according to the shrine history book. * '''{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kenhaya Susano no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Kushinada Hime|Q505469|Kushiinada Hime no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Fujiwara Tokihira|Q2994735|Fujiwara Tokihira Mikoto}}''' :According to legend, when {{jalink|Fujiwara no Moritsune|Q11623227}}, who was demoted in 4th year ({{jalink|1180|Q19702}}), settled in this area, he enshrined his ancestor, Fujiwara Tokihira, in his side hall. Since Tokihira is a political enemy of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (Tenjin), it is said that the parishioners of Tenjin did not visit the shrine . * '''{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Daisaku no Mikoto}}''' :{{jalink|Meiji period}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, a nearby mukakusha, was {{jalink|together enshrined|Q11412524}}. * '''{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Hondabetsu no Mikoto}}''' :In the Meiji period, Setsusha and Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. === History === According to the shrine's legend, the shrine history book was founded by the imperial order of {{jalink|Emperor Saga|Q316654}} during the {{jalink|Konin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810|Q23819}} to {{jalink|824|Q30353}}). . Like place names, the name ``Miyama'' appeared to be ``Miyama'' in ancient times, and was attributed to our company. {{jalink|Miyama clan|Q11355183}} served as {{jalink|priests|Q11589454}} for generations. ``Chiba Prefecture place name'' Miyama Village. .``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}} {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} states: Samukawa Shrine", but our company is said to be {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. It is also said that it was revered by the imperial court as {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}} of {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}} (however, {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} of {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}} is also called Ninomiya). It is unknown when it began to be called Ninomiya Shrine, but (Japan)|Q1327472|Inuigen}} 2 ({{jalink|1303|Q5614}}) The temple bell (stored in Shohoin, Narita City) says ``Soshu Ninomiya Shadan'', which suggests that this name already existed in the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}.{{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 19 years ({{jalink|1591|Q6766}}), {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} gave {{jalink|shuinji/black seals|Q11521520|shuinji}} 10 koku and , {{jalink|Edo Shogunate|Q205662}}The shogun made donations many times. {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}It is introduced in ``Narita Pilgrimage Record'' published in 1858 along with an illustration of ``Miyama Myo Shrine''. After entering {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1910, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Gensetsusha Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. Since ancient times, "Shirenshita" (area of parishioners) has consisted of 21.3 villages (currently {{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} eastern and northern areas, {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}, {{ja link|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}), and its influence can still be seen today at annual festivals. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The current shrine building was rebuilt during the {{jalink|Yasunaga}} years ({{jalink|1772|Q7690}} to {{jalink|1781|Q7719}}). Both the main shrine and worship hall have copper plate roofs, but the main shrine was built in {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}11 ({{jalink|1922|Q2165}})1 In October, the worship hall had its {{jalink|thatched roof|Q874531}} roof replaced in Taisho 14 ({{jalink|1925|Q18107}}). The floor plan of this shrine is {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}}, which was popular during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, but the floor height from {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}} to {{jalink|haiden|Q3076699}} is the same. In addition, the {{jalink|eaves|Q11637472}}'s {{jalink|kumimono|Q7814068}} and koshigumi were designed and constructed using the regular {{jalink|miya carpenter|Q65261787}} method of the time. In 1978 ({{jalink|1978|Q2483}}) {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}, it was designated as {{jalink|List of Funabashi City Designated Cultural Properties|Q11614137|Funabashi City's Tangible Cultural Properties}}. File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) haiden.JPG|Shrine of worshipFile:ninomiyajinnjya7.jpg|Mae Square File: Ninomiya.jpg|Photo of the shrine building in 1918 === Approach === [[File:Ninomiya-Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sando]] When you approach the shrine from the torii gate in front of the shrine, the approach becomes lower and then crosses the valley. Furthermore, a stream flows at the bottom of the valley, and the water vein is said to lead to a pond within the grounds of {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}}, which is enshrined in {{jalink|Tsudanuma|Q7849928}} in {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}. If you walk along the valley floor in a straight line, by the time you reach the top of the stone steps, you will be able to see the Karahafu facing the worship hall, and the master carpenter's {{jalink|Design|Q82604}} has been ingenious so that you can take your time to admire the large, majestic {{jalink|Karagohafu|Q977293}} curves and the majestic carvings. === Festival === * {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} (January 1st) * Oshahatsu Festival (January 7th) * Otsutsu porridge cooking ritual (January 14th) * {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} (February 3rd) * '''Regular Festival''' (October 16th) * '''Daikaage'' (Obiyo, {{jalink|lunar calendar|Q11511066}}night of November 13th) :A festival named after the bonfires lit by villagers to welcome Fujiwara no Moritsune and others who were said to have been exiled to this area. * '''{{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}}''' (Year of the Ox/Year of the Wild) :A festival held once every seven years, centered around our company, with nine shrines in Funabashi City, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}, {{jalink|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, and Narashino City participating. Our company plays the role of "father." === Surroundings === * '''{{jalink|Jinguji Temple (Funabashi City)|Q11589481|Douzan Jinguji Temple}}''' : Adjacent. Our company {{jalink|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}} before {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. === Cultural assets === === Chiba Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} - Designated on March 30, 2004. === Cultural property designated by Funabashi City === * Shrine building (tangible cultural property (building)) - Designated on February 27, 1992. * {{jalink|Saito Sonojo}} etc. dedicated haiku plaque (tangible cultural property (historical material)) - Designated on October 23, 1980. * Ninomiya Shrine Kagura (Intangible Folk Cultural Property) - Designated on June 26, 1995. * Ginkgo at Ninomiya Shrine (natural monument) - Designated on March 30, 2012. === Transportation access === * {{jalink|Tsudanuma Station|Q801534}} ({{jalink|East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|JR East}}{{jalink|Sobu Main Line|Q1191364}}) From the north exit ** Take the {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya Shrine" (ride time: 20 minutes) ** Take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya-jinja-mae" (20 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Yakuendai Station|Q8047226}} ({{jalink|Keisei Matsudo Line|Q3553734}}) ** Walk (3 minutes) from the Yakuendai Station Iriguchi bus stop on National Route 296, take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya-jinja-mae (10 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Keisei Okubo Station|Q6383851}} ({{jalink|Keisei Main Line|Q1063675}}) ** Walk (7 minutes) from the Toho Daigaku-mae bus stop, take {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya Shrine (ride time: 6 minutes) *: Keisei buses near Keisei Okubo Station have different routes for going down and going up. The nearest bus stop for going down is "Toho Daigaku-mae" and the nearest bus stop for going up is "Okubo Jujiro". === Source === {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System Chiba Prefecture Place Names" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Funabashi City Ninomiya Shrine Section * “Meiji, Taisho, and Showa eras preserved in postcard photographs” (Funabashi City Local Museum, March 23, 2005) * “Celebrating the 70th Anniversary of the Municipal System - 100 Years of Funabashi” (Kyoto Publishing, February 5, 2007) === Related items === * {{jalink|Mr. Miyama|Q11355183}} * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} * {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}} * {{jalink|Samukawa Shrine (Chiba City)|Q11457393|Samukawa Shrine}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}{{jalink|Chuo Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|Chuo Ward}}) - Like our company, Shikinai Shrine and Samukawa Shrine Ronsha * {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}}) - Another shrine named Shimousa Province Ninomiya * {{jalink|Gozu Tenno|Q11570247}} === External link === {{commonscat|Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi)}} * [http://www.ninomiya-jinja.jp Ninomiya Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ninomiya Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下総国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:嵯峨天皇]] [[Category:藤原時平]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}[[Category:qq]]

Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend・{{jalink|Honin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810 years|Q23819}}-{{jalink|824 years|Q30353}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Gongenzo|Q3155268}} |Other names = Samukawa Shrine, Miyama Myojin, Ninomiya Myojin|Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}} |Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Shimosa Sanzan Seven Year Festival|Q11361280}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ninomiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]]'''Ninomiya Shrine'' (Ninomiya Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} Miyama. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, tell them {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. In ancient times, it was also called ``Miyama Myojin'' or ``Ninomiya Myojin''. . === Enshrined God === The enshrined deity is listed according to the shrine history book. * '''{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kenhaya Susano no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Kushinada Hime|Q505469|Kushiinada Hime no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Fujiwara Tokihira|Q2994735|Fujiwara Tokihira Mikoto}}''' :According to legend, when {{jalink|Fujiwara no Moritsune|Q11623227}}, who was demoted in 4th year ({{jalink|1180|Q19702}}), settled in this area, he enshrined his ancestor, Fujiwara Tokihira, in his side hall. Since Tokihira is a political enemy of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (Tenjin), it is said that the parishioners of Tenjin did not visit the shrine . * '''{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Daisaku no Mikoto}}''' :{{jalink|Meiji period}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, a nearby mukakusha, was {{jalink|together enshrined|Q11412524}}. * '''{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Hondabetsu no Mikoto}}''' :In the Meiji period, Setsusha and Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. === History === According to the shrine's legend, the shrine history book was founded by the imperial order of {{jalink|Emperor Saga|Q316654}} during the {{jalink|Konin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810|Q23819}} to {{jalink|824|Q30353}}). . Like place names, the name ``Miyama'' appeared to be ``Miyama'' in ancient times, and was attributed to our company. {{jalink|Miyama clan|Q11355183}} served as {{jalink|priests|Q11589454}} for generations. ``Chiba Prefecture place name'' Miyama Village. .``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}} {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} states: Samukawa Shrine", but our company is said to be {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. It is also said that it was revered by the imperial court as {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}} of {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}} (however, {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} of {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}} is also called Ninomiya). It is unknown when it began to be called Ninomiya Shrine, but (Japan)|Q1327472|Inuigen}} 2 ({{jalink|1303|Q5614}}) The temple bell (stored in Shohoin, Narita City) says ``Soshu Ninomiya Shadan'', which suggests that this name already existed in the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}.{{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 19 years ({{jalink|1591|Q6766}}), {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} gave {{jalink|shuinji/black seals|Q11521520|shuinji}} 10 koku and , {{jalink|Edo Shogunate|Q205662}}The shogun made donations many times. {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}It is introduced in ``Narita Pilgrimage Record'' published in 1858 along with an illustration of ``Miyama Myo Shrine''. After entering {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1910, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Gensetsusha Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. Since ancient times, "Shirenshita" (area of parishioners) has consisted of 21.3 villages (currently {{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} eastern and northern areas, {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}, {{ja link|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}), and its influence can still be seen today at annual festivals. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The current shrine building was rebuilt during the {{jalink|Yasunaga}} years ({{jalink|1772|Q7690}} to {{jalink|1781|Q7719}}). Both the main shrine and worship hall have copper plate roofs, but the main shrine was built in {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}11 ({{jalink|1922|Q2165}})1 In October, the worship hall had its {{jalink|thatched roof|Q874531}} roof replaced in Taisho 14 ({{jalink|1925|Q18107}}). The floor plan of this shrine is {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}}, which was popular during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, but the floor height from {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}} to {{jalink|haiden|Q3076699}} is the same. In addition, the {{jalink|eaves|Q11637472}}'s {{jalink|kumimono|Q7814068}} and koshigumi were designed and constructed using the regular {{jalink|miya carpenter|Q65261787}} method of the time. In 1978 ({{jalink|1978|Q2483}}) {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}, it was designated as {{jalink|List of Funabashi City Designated Cultural Properties|Q11614137|Funabashi City's Tangible Cultural Properties}}. File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) haiden.JPG|Shrine of worshipFile:ninomiyajinnjya7.jpg|Mae Square File: Ninomiya.jpg|Photo of the shrine building in 1918 === Approach === [[File:Ninomiya-Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sando]] When you approach the shrine from the torii gate in front of the shrine, the approach becomes lower and then crosses the valley. Furthermore, a stream flows at the bottom of the valley, and the water vein is said to lead to a pond within the grounds of {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}}, which is enshrined in {{jalink|Tsudanuma|Q7849928}} in {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}. If you walk along the valley floor in a straight line, by the time you reach the top of the stone steps, you will be able to see the Karahafu facing the worship hall, and the master carpenter's {{jalink|Design|Q82604}} has been ingenious so that you can take your time to admire the large, majestic {{jalink|Karagohafu|Q977293}} curves and the majestic carvings. === Festival === * {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} (January 1st) * Oshahatsu Festival (January 7th) * Otsutsu porridge cooking ritual (January 14th) * {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} (February 3rd) * '''Regular Festival''' (October 16th) * '''Daikaage'' (Obiyo, {{jalink|lunar calendar|Q11511066}}night of November 13th) :A festival named after the bonfires lit by villagers to welcome Fujiwara no Moritsune and others who were said to have been exiled to this area. * '''{{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}}''' (Year of the Ox/Year of the Wild) :A festival held once every seven years, centered around our company, with nine shrines in Funabashi City, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}, {{jalink|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, and Narashino City participating. Our company plays the role of "father." === Surroundings === * '''{{jalink|Jinguji Temple (Funabashi City)|Q11589481|Douzan Jinguji Temple}}''' : Adjacent. Our company {{jalink|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}} before {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. === Cultural assets === === Chiba Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} - Designated on March 30, 2004. === Cultural property designated by Funabashi City === * Shrine building (tangible cultural property (building)) - Designated on February 27, 1992. * {{jalink|Saito Sonojo}} etc. dedicated haiku plaque (tangible cultural property (historical material)) - Designated on October 23, 1980. * Ninomiya Shrine Kagura (Intangible Folk Cultural Property) - Designated on June 26, 1995. * Ginkgo at Ninomiya Shrine (natural monument) - Designated on March 30, 2012. === Transportation access === * {{jalink|Tsudanuma Station|Q801534}} ({{jalink|East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|JR East}}{{jalink|Sobu Main Line|Q1191364}}) From the north exit ** Take the {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya Shrine" (ride time: 20 minutes) ** Take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya-jinja-mae" (20 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Yakuendai Station|Q8047226}} ({{jalink|Keisei Matsudo Line|Q3553734}}) ** Walk (3 minutes) from the Yakuendai Station Iriguchi bus stop on National Route 296, take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya-jinja-mae (10 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Keisei Okubo Station|Q6383851}} ({{jalink|Keisei Main Line|Q1063675}}) ** Walk (7 minutes) from the Toho Daigaku-mae bus stop, take {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya Shrine (ride time: 6 minutes) *: Keisei buses near Keisei Okubo Station have different routes for going down and going up. The nearest bus stop for going down is "Toho Daigaku-mae" and the nearest bus stop for going up is "Okubo Jujiro". === Source === {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System Chiba Prefecture Place Names" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Funabashi City Ninomiya Shrine Section * “Meiji, Taisho, and Showa eras preserved in postcard photographs” (Funabashi City Local Museum, March 23, 2005) * “Celebrating the 70th Anniversary of the Municipal System - 100 Years of Funabashi” (Kyoto Publishing, February 5, 2007) === Related items === * {{jalink|Mr. Miyama|Q11355183}} * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} * {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}} * {{jalink|Samukawa Shrine (Chiba City)|Q11457393|Samukawa Shrine}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}{{jalink|Chuo Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|Chuo Ward}}) - Like our company, Shikinai Shrine and Samukawa Shrine Ronsha * {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}}) - Another shrine named Shimousa Province Ninomiya * {{jalink|Gozu Tenno|Q11570247}} === External link === {{commonscat|Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi)}} * [http://www.ninomiya-jinja.jp Ninomiya Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ninomiya Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下総国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:嵯峨天皇]] [[Category:藤原時平]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}[[Category:qq]]

Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend・{{jalink|Honin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810 years|Q23819}}-{{jalink|824 years|Q30353}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Gongenzo|Q3155268}} |Other names = Samukawa Shrine, Miyama Myojin, Ninomiya Myojin|Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}} |Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Shimosa Sanzan Seven Year Festival|Q11361280}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ninomiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]]'''Ninomiya Shrine'' (Ninomiya Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} Miyama. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, tell them {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. In ancient times, it was also called ``Miyama Myojin'' or ``Ninomiya Myojin''. . === Enshrined God === The enshrined deity is listed according to the shrine history book. * '''{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kenhaya Susano no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Kushinada Hime|Q505469|Kushiinada Hime no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Fujiwara Tokihira|Q2994735|Fujiwara Tokihira Mikoto}}''' :According to legend, when {{jalink|Fujiwara no Moritsune|Q11623227}}, who was demoted in 4th year ({{jalink|1180|Q19702}}), settled in this area, he enshrined his ancestor, Fujiwara Tokihira, in his side hall. Since Tokihira is a political enemy of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (Tenjin), it is said that the parishioners of Tenjin did not visit the shrine . * '''{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Daisaku no Mikoto}}''' :{{jalink|Meiji period}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, a nearby mukakusha, was {{jalink|together enshrined|Q11412524}}. * '''{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Hondabetsu no Mikoto}}''' :In the Meiji period, Setsusha and Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. === History === According to the shrine's legend, the shrine history book was founded by the imperial order of {{jalink|Emperor Saga|Q316654}} during the {{jalink|Konin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810|Q23819}} to {{jalink|824|Q30353}}). . Like place names, the name ``Miyama'' appeared to be ``Miyama'' in ancient times, and was attributed to our company. {{jalink|Miyama clan|Q11355183}} served as {{jalink|priests|Q11589454}} for generations. ``Chiba Prefecture place name'' Miyama Village. .``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}} {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} states: Samukawa Shrine", but our company is said to be {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. It is also said that it was revered by the imperial court as {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}} of {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}} (however, {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} of {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}} is also called Ninomiya). It is unknown when it began to be called Ninomiya Shrine, but (Japan)|Q1327472|Inuigen}} 2 ({{jalink|1303|Q5614}}) The temple bell (stored in Shohoin, Narita City) says ``Soshu Ninomiya Shadan'', which suggests that this name already existed in the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}.{{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 19 years ({{jalink|1591|Q6766}}), {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} gave {{jalink|shuinji/black seals|Q11521520|shuinji}} 10 koku and , {{jalink|Edo Shogunate|Q205662}}The shogun made donations many times. {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}It is introduced in ``Narita Pilgrimage Record'' published in 1858 along with an illustration of ``Miyama Myo Shrine''. After entering {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1910, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Gensetsusha Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. Since ancient times, "Shirenshita" (area of parishioners) has consisted of 21.3 villages (currently {{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} eastern and northern areas, {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}, {{ja link|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}), and its influence can still be seen today at annual festivals. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The current shrine building was rebuilt during the {{jalink|Yasunaga}} years ({{jalink|1772|Q7690}} to {{jalink|1781|Q7719}}). Both the main shrine and worship hall have copper plate roofs, but the main shrine was built in {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}11 ({{jalink|1922|Q2165}})1 In October, the worship hall had its {{jalink|thatched roof|Q874531}} roof replaced in Taisho 14 ({{jalink|1925|Q18107}}). The floor plan of this shrine is {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}}, which was popular during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, but the floor height from {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}} to {{jalink|haiden|Q3076699}} is the same. In addition, the {{jalink|eaves|Q11637472}}'s {{jalink|kumimono|Q7814068}} and koshigumi were designed and constructed using the regular {{jalink|miya carpenter|Q65261787}} method of the time. In 1978 ({{jalink|1978|Q2483}}) {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}, it was designated as {{jalink|List of Funabashi City Designated Cultural Properties|Q11614137|Funabashi City's Tangible Cultural Properties}}. File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) haiden.JPG|Shrine of worshipFile:ninomiyajinnjya7.jpg|Mae Square File: Ninomiya.jpg|Photo of the shrine building in 1918 === Approach === [[File:Ninomiya-Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sando]] When you approach the shrine from the torii gate in front of the shrine, the approach becomes lower and then crosses the valley. Furthermore, a stream flows at the bottom of the valley, and the water vein is said to lead to a pond within the grounds of {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}}, which is enshrined in {{jalink|Tsudanuma|Q7849928}} in {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}. If you walk along the valley floor in a straight line, by the time you reach the top of the stone steps, you will be able to see the Karahafu facing the worship hall, and the master carpenter's {{jalink|Design|Q82604}} has been ingenious so that you can take your time to admire the large, majestic {{jalink|Karagohafu|Q977293}} curves and the majestic carvings. === Festival === * {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} (January 1st) * Oshahatsu Festival (January 7th) * Otsutsu porridge cooking ritual (January 14th) * {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} (February 3rd) * '''Regular Festival''' (October 16th) * '''Daikaage'' (Obiyo, {{jalink|lunar calendar|Q11511066}}night of November 13th) :A festival named after the bonfires lit by villagers to welcome Fujiwara no Moritsune and others who were said to have been exiled to this area. * '''{{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}}''' (Year of the Ox/Year of the Wild) :A festival held once every seven years, centered around our company, with nine shrines in Funabashi City, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}, {{jalink|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, and Narashino City participating. Our company plays the role of "father." === Surroundings === * '''{{jalink|Jinguji Temple (Funabashi City)|Q11589481|Douzan Jinguji Temple}}''' : Adjacent. Our company {{jalink|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}} before {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. === Cultural assets === === Chiba Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} - Designated on March 30, 2004. === Cultural property designated by Funabashi City === * Shrine building (tangible cultural property (building)) - Designated on February 27, 1992. * {{jalink|Saito Sonojo}} etc. dedicated haiku plaque (tangible cultural property (historical material)) - Designated on October 23, 1980. * Ninomiya Shrine Kagura (Intangible Folk Cultural Property) - Designated on June 26, 1995. * Ginkgo at Ninomiya Shrine (natural monument) - Designated on March 30, 2012. === Transportation access === * {{jalink|Tsudanuma Station|Q801534}} ({{jalink|East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|JR East}}{{jalink|Sobu Main Line|Q1191364}}) From the north exit ** Take the {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya Shrine" (ride time: 20 minutes) ** Take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya-jinja-mae" (20 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Yakuendai Station|Q8047226}} ({{jalink|Keisei Matsudo Line|Q3553734}}) ** Walk (3 minutes) from the Yakuendai Station Iriguchi bus stop on National Route 296, take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya-jinja-mae (10 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Keisei Okubo Station|Q6383851}} ({{jalink|Keisei Main Line|Q1063675}}) ** Walk (7 minutes) from the Toho Daigaku-mae bus stop, take {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya Shrine (ride time: 6 minutes) *: Keisei buses near Keisei Okubo Station have different routes for going down and going up. The nearest bus stop for going down is "Toho Daigaku-mae" and the nearest bus stop for going up is "Okubo Jujiro". === Source === {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System Chiba Prefecture Place Names" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Funabashi City Ninomiya Shrine Section * “Meiji, Taisho, and Showa eras preserved in postcard photographs” (Funabashi City Local Museum, March 23, 2005) * “Celebrating the 70th Anniversary of the Municipal System - 100 Years of Funabashi” (Kyoto Publishing, February 5, 2007) === Related items === * {{jalink|Mr. Miyama|Q11355183}} * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} * {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}} * {{jalink|Samukawa Shrine (Chiba City)|Q11457393|Samukawa Shrine}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}{{jalink|Chuo Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|Chuo Ward}}) - Like our company, Shikinai Shrine and Samukawa Shrine Ronsha * {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}}) - Another shrine named Shimousa Province Ninomiya * {{jalink|Gozu Tenno|Q11570247}} === External link === {{commonscat|Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi)}} * [http://www.ninomiya-jinja.jp Ninomiya Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ninomiya Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下総国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:嵯峨天皇]] [[Category:藤原時平]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}[[Category:qq]]

[[JA:二宮神社 (船橋市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11371511}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ninomiya Shrine |Image = [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) shaden.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}}Miyama 5-20-1 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kenhaya Susano no Mikoto}}

Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend・{{jalink|Honin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810 years|Q23819}}-{{jalink|824 years|Q30353}}) |Main shrine = {{jalink|Gongenzo|Q3155268}} |Other names = Samukawa Shrine, Miyama Myojin, Ninomiya Myojin|Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}} |Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Shimosa Sanzan Seven Year Festival|Q11361280}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ninomiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]]'''Ninomiya Shrine'' (Ninomiya Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} Miyama. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}{{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, tell them {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. In ancient times, it was also called ``Miyama Myojin'' or ``Ninomiya Myojin''. . === Enshrined God === The enshrined deity is listed according to the shrine history book. * '''{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kenhaya Susano no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Kushinada Hime|Q505469|Kushiinada Hime no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Fujiwara Tokihira|Q2994735|Fujiwara Tokihira Mikoto}}''' :According to legend, when {{jalink|Fujiwara no Moritsune|Q11623227}}, who was demoted in 4th year ({{jalink|1180|Q19702}}), settled in this area, he enshrined his ancestor, Fujiwara Tokihira, in his side hall. Since Tokihira is a political enemy of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (Tenjin), it is said that the parishioners of Tenjin did not visit the shrine . * '''{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Daisaku no Mikoto}}''' :{{jalink|Meiji period}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, a nearby mukakusha, was {{jalink|together enshrined|Q11412524}}. * '''{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Hondabetsu no Mikoto}}''' :In the Meiji period, Setsusha and Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. === History === According to the shrine's legend, the shrine history book was founded by the imperial order of {{jalink|Emperor Saga|Q316654}} during the {{jalink|Konin|Q1373070}} years ({{jalink|810|Q23819}} to {{jalink|824|Q30353}}). . Like place names, the name ``Miyama'' appeared to be ``Miyama'' in ancient times, and was attributed to our company. {{jalink|Miyama clan|Q11355183}} served as {{jalink|priests|Q11589454}} for generations. ``Chiba Prefecture place name'' Miyama Village. .``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' from the middle of the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}} {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} states: Samukawa Shrine", but our company is said to be {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. It is also said that it was revered by the imperial court as {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Ninomiya}} of {{jalink|Shimosa Province|Q1053695}} (however, {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} of {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}} is also called Ninomiya). It is unknown when it began to be called Ninomiya Shrine, but (Japan)|Q1327472|Inuigen}} 2 ({{jalink|1303|Q5614}}) The temple bell (stored in Shohoin, Narita City) says ``Soshu Ninomiya Shadan'', which suggests that this name already existed in the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}.{{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 19 years ({{jalink|1591|Q6766}}), {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}} gave {{jalink|shuinji/black seals|Q11521520|shuinji}} 10 koku and , {{jalink|Edo Shogunate|Q205662}}The shogun made donations many times. {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}It is introduced in ``Narita Pilgrimage Record'' published in 1858 along with an illustration of ``Miyama Myo Shrine''. After entering {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1910, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Gensetsusha Awa Hachiman Shrine were enshrined together. Since ancient times, "Shirenshita" (area of parishioners) has consisted of 21.3 villages (currently {{jalink|Funabashi City|Q271417}} eastern and northern areas, {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}, {{ja link|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}), and its influence can still be seen today at annual festivals. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The current shrine building was rebuilt during the {{jalink|Yasunaga}} years ({{jalink|1772|Q7690}} to {{jalink|1781|Q7719}}). Both the main shrine and worship hall have copper plate roofs, but the main shrine was built in {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}11 ({{jalink|1922|Q2165}})1 In October, the worship hall had its {{jalink|thatched roof|Q874531}} roof replaced in Taisho 14 ({{jalink|1925|Q18107}}). The floor plan of this shrine is {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}}, which was popular during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, but the floor height from {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}} to {{jalink|haiden|Q3076699}} is the same. In addition, the {{jalink|eaves|Q11637472}}'s {{jalink|kumimono|Q7814068}} and koshigumi were designed and constructed using the regular {{jalink|miya carpenter|Q65261787}} method of the time. In 1978 ({{jalink|1978|Q2483}}) {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}, it was designated as {{jalink|List of Funabashi City Designated Cultural Properties|Q11614137|Funabashi City's Tangible Cultural Properties}}. File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi) haiden.JPG|Shrine of worshipFile:ninomiyajinnjya7.jpg|Mae Square File: Ninomiya.jpg|Photo of the shrine building in 1918 === Approach === [[File:Ninomiya-Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sando]] When you approach the shrine from the torii gate in front of the shrine, the approach becomes lower and then crosses the valley. Furthermore, a stream flows at the bottom of the valley, and the water vein is said to lead to a pond within the grounds of {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}}, which is enshrined in {{jalink|Tsudanuma|Q7849928}} in {{jalink|Narashino City|Q318047}}. If you walk along the valley floor in a straight line, by the time you reach the top of the stone steps, you will be able to see the Karahafu facing the worship hall, and the master carpenter's {{jalink|Design|Q82604}} has been ingenious so that you can take your time to admire the large, majestic {{jalink|Karagohafu|Q977293}} curves and the majestic carvings. === Festival === * {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} (January 1st) * Oshahatsu Festival (January 7th) * Otsutsu porridge cooking ritual (January 14th) * {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} (February 3rd) * '''Regular Festival''' (October 16th) * '''Daikaage'' (Obiyo, {{jalink|lunar calendar|Q11511066}}night of November 13th) :A festival named after the bonfires lit by villagers to welcome Fujiwara no Moritsune and others who were said to have been exiled to this area. * '''{{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}}''' (Year of the Ox/Year of the Wild) :A festival held once every seven years, centered around our company, with nine shrines in Funabashi City, {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}, {{jalink|Yachiyo City|Q328651}}, and Narashino City participating. Our company plays the role of "father." === Surroundings === * '''{{jalink|Jinguji Temple (Funabashi City)|Q11589481|Douzan Jinguji Temple}}''' : Adjacent. Our company {{jalink|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}} before {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. === Cultural assets === === Chiba Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} - Designated on March 30, 2004. === Cultural property designated by Funabashi City === * Shrine building (tangible cultural property (building)) - Designated on February 27, 1992. * {{jalink|Saito Sonojo}} etc. dedicated haiku plaque (tangible cultural property (historical material)) - Designated on October 23, 1980. * Ninomiya Shrine Kagura (Intangible Folk Cultural Property) - Designated on June 26, 1995. * Ginkgo at Ninomiya Shrine (natural monument) - Designated on March 30, 2012. === Transportation access === * {{jalink|Tsudanuma Station|Q801534}} ({{jalink|East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|JR East}}{{jalink|Sobu Main Line|Q1191364}}) From the north exit ** Take the {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya Shrine" (ride time: 20 minutes) ** Take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, "Ninomiya-jinja-mae" (20 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Yakuendai Station|Q8047226}} ({{jalink|Keisei Matsudo Line|Q3553734}}) ** Walk (3 minutes) from the Yakuendai Station Iriguchi bus stop on National Route 296, take the {{jalink|Funabashi Shinkeisei Bus}} (Tsu 08 bound for Ninomiya Jinja-mae) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya-jinja-mae (10 minute ride) * From {{jalink|Keisei Okubo Station|Q6383851}} ({{jalink|Keisei Main Line|Q1063675}}) ** Walk (7 minutes) from the Toho Daigaku-mae bus stop, take {{jalink|Keisei Bus|Q11374367}} (Tsu 02 bound for Ninomiya Shrine) and get off at the final stop, Ninomiya Shrine (ride time: 6 minutes) *: Keisei buses near Keisei Okubo Station have different routes for going down and going up. The nearest bus stop for going down is "Toho Daigaku-mae" and the nearest bus stop for going up is "Okubo Jujiro". === Source === {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System Chiba Prefecture Place Names" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Funabashi City Ninomiya Shrine Section * “Meiji, Taisho, and Showa eras preserved in postcard photographs” (Funabashi City Local Museum, March 23, 2005) * “Celebrating the 70th Anniversary of the Municipal System - 100 Years of Funabashi” (Kyoto Publishing, February 5, 2007) === Related items === * {{jalink|Mr. Miyama|Q11355183}} * {{jalink|Seven Year Festival of Shimousa Sanzan|Q11361280}} * {{jalink|Kikuta Shrine|Q11619900}} * {{jalink|Samukawa Shrine (Chiba City)|Q11457393|Samukawa Shrine}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Chiba City|Q170616}}{{jalink|Chuo Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|Chuo Ward}}) - Like our company, Shikinai Shrine and Samukawa Shrine Ronsha * {{jalink|Tamazaki Shrine|Q11572538}} (Chiba Prefecture {{jalink|Asahi City|Q637868}}) - Another shrine named Shimousa Province Ninomiya * {{jalink|Gozu Tenno|Q11570247}} === External link === {{commonscat|Ninomiya-jinja (Funabashi)}} * [http://www.ninomiya-jinja.jp Ninomiya Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ninomiya Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:船橋市の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:下総国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:嵯峨天皇]] [[Category:藤原時平]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNinomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Ninomiya Shrine (Q11371511)}}[[Category:qq]]
